_about: Packet Runner routine configuration TEMPLATE (PRD §8.5A). One version-controlled config per routine. Copy to routine.config.json and replace every <PLACEHOLDER>. Secret VALUES never live here — only safe aliases (PRD §8.9). Preflight fails closed before any packet mutation when this config is missing, inconsistent, points at an inaccessible project/repo/brief/qualification/evidence destination, allows overlapping runs, or uses contract/schema versions outside the supported range (PRD §8.5A configuration validation).

provider._note: PRD §8.5A + FR-9. The provider must satisfy all nine capabilities; selection evidence is recorded under provider_capability_results below. Do NOT build a generalized adapter framework for v1.
provider.name: <PROVIDER_NAME e.g. claude-code | cursor | codex>

self_pause_action._note: Machine-callable self-pause/disable action OR a deterministic provider-native fail-closed latch, scoped to THIS routine only (PRD §8.5A, §8.13). Packet Runner may pause/disable within an incident boundary but may NEVER re-enable itself.

_timezone_note: PRD §8.5A: America/Chicago for the initial routine unless Isaiah explicitly changes it. Used for Execution Window evaluation when a window embeds no timezone (§8.1).
_schedule_note: INTENTIONALLY null. PRD §8.5A + FR-8: schedule is an operator-supplied provider-native schedule, and NO unattended run is enabled until this value is reviewed. UNATTENDED RUNS STAY DISABLED until reviewed AND until the §8.20 20-cycle production-qualification gate passes with explicit operator sign-off. Until then, execution is manual Run-Now only, under the same provider non-overlap guard. Replace null with the reviewed provider-native cron/recurrence ONLY after qualification + authorization.

schedule_fields_when_enabled._note: Reference shape only — NOT active while "schedule": null. Populate the live "schedule" field above (provider-native) after review. Missed runs are NOT backfilled (FR-8). Time zone / daylight-saving / manual-run behavior remain provider-native.

project_id: <NOTION_PROJECT_PAGE_ID — the PROJECT relation target scoping the QUEUE query (PRD §6.2, FR-2)>

repository._note: PRD §8.5A, §8.21. Never modify the canonical default branch directly in unattended v1; use a provider-isolated clone/branch/worktree and a packet branch named packet/<PKT-ID>-<short-slug>.
repository.name: <REPO_NAME>

_brief_page_note: Created 2026-06-23 ('Packet Runner Brief — v1 Pilot', under the PRD page) per PRD §8.6. Single canonical latest-brief page; preflight fails visibly if unresolvable/un-updatable. Operator may relocate/redesignate for the real pilot project.
_qualification_evidence_page_note: Created 2026-06-23 ('Packet Runner Qualification — v1 Pilot', under the PRD page) per PRD §8.20. Required only while the qualification pilot is active.

durable_evidence_destination._note: PRD §8.5A + §8.18. Stable Notion page/file area OR repository evidence path for redacted incident exports and portable review artifacts. MUST NOT be an ephemeral local directory.

_max_packets_note: PRD §8.5A + §6.6: pilot is exactly 1 packet per cycle. Increasing this is a persistent change requiring review (§8.13) and may expand only one dimension at a time after qualification (§8.20).
cycle_timeout_seconds: <INT — MUST be shorter than the schedule interval (PRD §8.5A)>
packet_timeout_seconds: <INT — MUST be shorter than the 14400s (4h) stale-FOCUS threshold (PRD §8.5A, §8.14)>
_maintenance_note: PRD §8.5A + §8.17. Bounded cleanup/archival runs AFTER packet reconciliation and BEFORE the brief; it starts no mission work so it does not count against max_packets_per_cycle, but must stay time-bounded.

model._note: PRD §8.5A, FR-18, §8.13. Session-local model choice is allowed only within this allowlist; changing the default or allowlist is a persistent change requiring review.

_execution_modes_note: PRD §6 + FR-9: fresh executor worker preferred; inline executor fallback. Both must be approved for the controller to switch between them session-locally (§8.13).

connections._note: PRD §8.5A + §8.9. Core tool/MCP connections and SAFE credential aliases only — never secret values. Least-privilege (FR-13). Aliases such as github:primary, notion:primary, STRIPE_API_KEY are referenced; values stay in native secret stores.

authorized_identities._note: PRD §8.5A + §8.10 + §8.13. Reviewer/operator identities and who holds provider pause/re-enable authority. Re-enablement of a paused routine is an explicit operator decision; the routine may never re-enable itself.

provider_capability_results._note: PRD §8.5A provider-selection rule + FR-9. Record VERIFIED evidence per capability. Every capability except session_export is REQUIRED; session_export may be 'fallback' using the §8.18 compact-bundle path. Fail closed: any unverified capability cell blocks rollout (PROGRAM_PLAN honest gate map). Preflight P9–P12 re-asserts these live.
provider_capability_results.non_overlap_enforcement: <verified | BLOCKED: requires real provider integration testing>
provider_capability_results.repository_isolation: <verified | BLOCKED: requires real provider integration testing>
provider_capability_results.worker_or_inline_execution: <verified | BLOCKED: requires real provider integration testing>
provider_capability_results.registry_and_mcp_access: <verified | BLOCKED: requires real provider integration testing>
provider_capability_results.native_execution_reference: <verified | BLOCKED: requires real provider integration testing>
provider_capability_results.failure_signaling: <verified | BLOCKED: requires real provider integration testing>
provider_capability_results.routine_pause_or_fail_closed_latch: <verified | BLOCKED: requires real provider integration testing>
provider_capability_results.completion_notification_with_brief_link: <verified | BLOCKED: requires real provider integration testing>
provider_capability_results.session_export: <verified | fallback (§8.18 compact bundle) | BLOCKED: requires real provider integration testing>

contract_versions._note: PRD §8.5A. Preflight rejects any version outside the supported range (also enforced at dispatch per §8.4).

_qualification_note: PRD §8.20. Set true only during the scheduled 20-cycle qualification pilot; while true, every cycle appends a qualification-evidence row after brief+notification results are known. A missing/unverifiable row makes the cycle non-qualifying and breaks the streak even when cycle health is HEALTHY. Production-qualified status requires 20 consecutive clean cycles + operator sign-off; scheduling stays OFF until then.
